tool.name: TSENAT
tool.description: Tsallis Entropy Analysis for transcript-level isoform complexity
tool.version_command: echo $(R --version | grep version | head -n 1) && Rscript -e "library('TSENAT'); cat('TSENAT', packageVersion('TSENAT'), sep='\\n')"

regex.description: An undefined error occurred, please check your input carefully and contact your administrator.
An undefined error occurred, please check your input carefully and contact your administrator.

tool.command: Rscript '${__tool_directory__}/tsenat.R' --salmon_dir '${salmon_dir}' --metadata '${metadata}' --annotation '${annotation}' --sample_col '${sample_col}' --condition_col '${condition_col}' --subject_col '${subject_col}' #if str($paired_design.paired) == 'yes' --paired #end if --control '${control}' --q_min $q_parameters.q_min --q_max $q_parameters.q_max --q_step $q_parameters.q_step --filter_stringency '${filter_stringency}' --p_threshold $statistical_thresholds.p_threshold --fdr_threshold $statistical_thresholds.fdr_threshold --significance_threshold $statistical_thresholds.significance_threshold --bootstrap $bootstrap_options.bootstrap --nboot $bootstrap_options.nboot --bootstrap_method '${bootstrap_options.bootstrap_method}' --bootstrap_ci $bootstrap_options.bootstrap_ci --bootstrap_include_diagnostics $bootstrap_options.bootstrap_include_diagnostics --norm $normalization_options.norm #if str($normalization_options.norm_method) != 'none' --norm_method '${normalization_options.norm_method}' #end if --pseudocount $normalization_options.pseudocount --shrinkage '${normalization_options.shrinkage}' --min_valid_frac $normalization_options.min_valid_frac --sait_method '${method_selection.sait_method}' --sait_pcorr '${method_selection.sait_pcorr}' --sait_jis_fdr $method_selection.jis_use_sait_fdr --divergence_ci $method_selection.divergence_ci --assumptions_checks '${method_selection.assumptions_checks}' #if str($method_selection.sait_method) == 'gee' --corstr '${method_selection.gee_corstr}' #end if #if str($method_selection.multicorr_method) != 'default' --multicorr '${method_selection.multicorr_method}' #end if #if str($normalization_options.reference_group) != '' --reference_group '${normalization_options.reference_group}' #end if #if str($divergence_section.control_group) != '' --control_group '${divergence_section.control_group}' #end if #if $advanced_options.enable_gene_subsetting --subset_n_genes $advanced_options.subset_n_genes --subset_select_by '${advanced_options.subset_select_by}' --subset_seed $advanced_options.subset_seed #end if #if str($filtering_options.min_tpm) != '' --min_tpm $filtering_options.min_tpm #end if #if str($filtering_options.min_isoform_abundance) != '' --min_isoform_abundance $filtering_options.min_isoform_abundance #end if #if str($normalization_options.what_output) != 'S' --what '${normalization_options.what_output}' #end if #if $method_selection.srh_advanced --wy_randomizations $method_selection.wy_randomizations --eta2_threshold_moderate $method_selection.eta2_threshold_moderate --eta2_threshold_strong $method_selection.eta2_threshold_strong --min_nperm $method_selection.min_nperm --max_nperm $method_selection.max_nperm #end if #if $advanced_options.enable_m_estimator --loss_type '${advanced_options.m_estimator_loss_type}' --max_iter $advanced_options.m_estimator_max_iter --m_tol $advanced_options.m_estimator_tol --q_combine_method '${advanced_options.m_estimator_q_combine}' --influence_threshold $advanced_options.m_estimator_influence --scale_method '${advanced_options.m_estimator_scale_method}' #end if --nthreads $performance.nthreads #if str($advanced_options.enabled) == 'yes' --output_prefix '${advanced_options.output_prefix}' #if $advanced_options.save_intermediates --save_intermediates #end if #else --output_prefix 'tsenat' #end if --output_dir './'

param.name: salmon_dir
param.label: Salmon quantification directory
param.help: Directory containing Salmon quantification output with subdirectories for each sample, each containing a quant.sf file (Salmon output format). Sample directory names must exactly match the 'sample' column in metadata file (case-sensitive).

param.name: metadata
param.label: Sample metadata
param.help: TSV file with required columns: 'sample' (sample IDs matching Salmon directories), 'condition' (experimental conditions). Optional column for paired designs: 'subject_col' or 'paired_samples' (groups paired/repeated samples). Example: sample\tcondition\tpaired_samples; SRR14800481\tnormal\tA; SRR14800479\ttumor\tA

param.name: annotation
param.label: Transcript annotation
param.help: GFF3 annotation file with transcript ID to gene ID mapping (e.g., GENCODE GFF3 filtered to your genes of interest). Used to group transcripts by gene and map annotation information.

param.name: sample_col
param.label: Sample column name
param.help: Column name in metadata identifying sample IDs

validator.message: Column name must start with a letter or underscore, and contain only letters, numbers, underscores, and dots

param.name: condition_col
param.label: Condition column name
param.help: Column name in metadata identifying experimental conditions (e.g., treatment groups)

validator.message: Column name must start with a letter or underscore, and contain only letters, numbers, underscores, and dots

param.name: control
param.label: Reference condition
param.help: (Optional) Reference/control condition for contrast specification. If empty, first level used.

param.name: subject_col
param.label: Subject/pairing column name
param.help: Column in metadata that groups paired samples (e.g., patient ID)

validator.message: Column name must start with a letter or underscore, and contain only letters, numbers, underscores, and dots

param.name: q_min
param.label: Minimum q value
param.help: Minimum entropic index q. q=0 emphasizes rare isoforms (richness); q=1 gives Shannon entropy (balanced diversity); q→0 detects if diversity is maintained or collapsed across rare variants.

param.name: q_max
param.label: Maximum q value
param.help: Maximum entropic index q. q=2 emphasizes dominant isoforms (Simpson index); high q is robust to rare variants and detects dominance shifts. Examine q-curves showing how results change across scales.

param.name: q_step
param.label: q step size
param.help: Resolution of q-spectrum (smaller = more q-values, higher resolution curves, slower). Default 0.05 creates 41 q-values from 0 to 2. Smaller steps (e.g., 0.02) provide smoother q-curves for visualizing scale-dependent patterns.

param.name: filter_stringency
param.label: Low-abundance filter stringency
param.help: Filter strategy for low-abundance transcripts. Soft: ≥25% presence in samples (preserve isoform diversity). Medium: ≥50% presence with TPM above median (balances noise reduction and diversity, recommended). Severe: ≥75% presence with high TPM (focus on abundant isoforms only). Choice affects entropy estimates: severe reduces noise but may miss rare isoforms and reduce diversity signal.

param.name: p_threshold
param.label: P-value threshold
param.help: Uncorrected p-value threshold for individual statistical tests (e.g., Wilcoxon tests between conditions). Typical: 0.05. Note: Used before multiple testing correction (see p-value correction methods).

param.name: fdr_threshold
param.label: FDR threshold
param.help: False Discovery Rate (FDR) adjusted p-value threshold for controlling Type I error across multiple tests. Benjamini-Hochberg correction recommended. Typical: 0.05. Genes with adjusted p < FDR threshold are considered significant at specified false discovery rate.

param.name: significance_threshold
param.label: General significance threshold
param.help: General significance level applied across all statistical tests when no specific correction is specified. Typical: 0.05 (5% Type I error rate). May also apply to interaction tests (q × condition effects).

param.name: bootstrap
param.label: Enable bootstrap
param.help: Enable bootstrap resampling to compute robust confidence intervals for entropy estimates. Adds computational cost but provides non-parametric uncertainty quantification. Recommended for small sample sizes or non-normal data.

param.name: nboot
param.label: Bootstrap replicates
param.help: Number of bootstrap resampling iterations (default: 1000). Larger values (e.g., 5000) improve CI precision but increase runtime. Use ≥1000 for final analyses; 100-500 for exploratory work.

param.name: bootstrap_method
param.label: Bootstrap method
param.help: Bootstrap CI method. Percentile: simple, fast, less accurate for skewed distributions. BCa: bias-corrected & accelerated, more accurate and recommended for entropy estimates which may be skewed.

param.name: bootstrap_ci
param.label: Bootstrap CI level
param.help: Confidence level for CIs (0.95 = 95% CI). Narrower intervals (e.g., 0.90) are easier to achieve; wider intervals (e.g., 0.99) provide stronger confidence.

param.name: bootstrap_include_diagnostics
param.label: Include bootstrap diagnostics
param.help: Save bootstrap diagnostics including convergence checks, variance ratios, and CI coverage validations. Useful for assessing bootstrap stability and data quality.

param.name: norm
param.label: Enable normalization
param.help: Normalize entropy estimates to [0,1] range for comparable cross-q assessment. Recommended (default: enabled) for comparing entropy across different q-values and between genes. Disable only if raw unnormalized entropy is needed.

param.name: norm_method
param.label: Normalization method
param.help: Method for normalization. Default (automatic): chooses algorithm based on data. Range: [0,1] scaling. Z-score: centered and scaled. Quantile: quantile normalization for cross-sample comparison.

param.name: pseudocount
param.label: Pseudocount
param.help: Add pseudocount to break ties and handle zeros in sparse RNA-seq data. Default: 0 (automatic selection). Typical: 0.5-1. Critical for rank-based non-parametric tests (e.g., Scheirer-Ray-Hare) which require unique orderings. Scales pseudocount by median library size.

param.name: shrinkage
param.label: Shrinkage method
param.help: Shrinkage estimation for robust variance estimation. None: direct estimation. James-Stein: empirical Bayes shrinkage toward grand mean. Use James-Stein for small sample sizes or high-variance data.

param.name: min_valid_frac
param.label: Minimum valid fraction
param.help: Minimum fraction of valid (non-missing) values required to retain a transcript or gene (default: 0.75 = 75%). Transcripts with <75% valid samples are excluded due to insufficient data quality. Increase for stringent filtering; decrease to retain more transcripts.

param.name: reference_group
param.label: Reference group for normalization
param.help: (Optional) Reference group identifier for 'relative_reference' normalization method. Entropy values will be normalized relative to this group's mean. If empty, uses first condition group.

param.name: what_output
param.label: Diversity output type (advanced)
param.help: Output type for diversity calculation: 'S' = entropy (default) or 'D' = diversity (Hill numbers). Most users should use default entropy ('S'). Choose 'D' only if you specifically need diversity measures.

param.name: sait_method
param.label: SAIT fitting method
param.help: Statistical method for Scale-Adaptive Interaction Testing (SAIT) of q × condition effects. GAM (recommended): Semi-parametric model with flexible smooth functions; best for non-linear q-curves. LMM: Linear mixed model with AR(1) correlation; good for repeated measures. FPCA: Functional PCA; reduces dimensionality for high q-resolution. GEE: Generalized estimating equations; robust to misspecified correlation structure.

param.name: sait_pcorr
param.label: SAIT p-value correction
param.help: Multiple testing correction for q × condition interaction tests (corrects for ~3089 transcripts). Benjamini-Hochberg (recommended): Controls FDR, most powerful for many tests. Bonferroni: Strict, very conservative. Hochberg: Step-up method, more powerful than Bonferroni. Holm: Step-down, better rejection power.

param.name: multicorr_method
param.label: Advanced multiple correction for SAIT
param.help: Advanced multiple comparison correction methods for SAIT interaction tests. Default uses primary p-value correction. Hochberg: More powerful than Bonferroni. Westfall-Young: Bootstrap-based, controls family-wise error rate. Benjamini-Yekutieli: Robust FDR control under dependence.

param.name: gee_corstr
param.label: GEE correlation structure
param.help: Correlation structure for Generalized Estimating Equations (only applicable if SAIT method is GEE). AR(1): Auto-regressive order 1 (good for q-spectrum). Exchangeable: treats all q-values as equally correlated. Independence: assumes independence (less rigid).

param.name: jis_use_sait_fdr
param.label: Use SAIT FDR for jackknife switching detection
param.help: Use SAIT FDR threshold to filter genes before jackknife isoform switching analysis. Enables two-stage approach: (1) identify genes with significant q × condition interaction, (2) detect transcripts with robust switching patterns within those genes. Recommended for specificity.

param.name: srh_advanced
param.label: Enable Scheirer-Ray-Hare advanced options
param.help: Enable advanced tuning for Scheirer-Ray-Hare rank-based test (Westfall-Young permutations, effect size thresholds, permutation bounds).

param.name: wy_randomizations
param.label: Westfall-Young randomizations
param.help: Number of permutations for Westfall-Young multiple testing correction (default: 500). Higher values improve statistical accuracy but increase computation time. Use 'auto' mode to estimate from data.

param.name: eta2_threshold_moderate
param.label: Moderate effect size threshold
param.help: Effect size (eta-squared) threshold for moderate effects (default: 0.01). Used to classify interaction strength: <0.01 = small, 0.01-0.1 = moderate, >0.1 = strong.

param.name: eta2_threshold_strong
param.label: Strong effect size threshold
param.help: Effect size (eta-squared) threshold for strong effects (default: 0.1). Classification: <0.01 = small, 0.01-0.1 = moderate, >0.1 = strong effects.

param.name: min_nperm
param.label: Minimum permutations
param.help: Minimum number of permutations for rank-based test (default: 100). Lower bound for adaptive permutation testing.

param.name: max_nperm
param.label: Maximum permutations
param.help: Maximum number of permutations for rank-based test (default: 10000). Upper bound for adaptive permutation testing.

param.name: divergence_ci
param.label: Divergence CI level
param.help: Confidence level for Tsallis divergence estimates measuring information-theoretic distance between conditions (default: 0.95 = 95% CI). Divergence measures scale-dependent differences in isoform complexity between groups.

param.name: assumptions_checks
param.label: Assumptions validation
param.help: Statistical assumption checks to perform. All checks: comprehensive validation of normality, homoscedasticity, independence (best for diagnosis). Rank-based only: robustness validation using non-parametric Scheirer-Ray-Hare tests. GAM only: semi-parametric diagnostics.

param.name: min_tpm
param.label: Minimum TPM threshold
param.help: (Optional) Minimum TPM value for transcript filtering (default: 1.0 if not specified). Transcripts with TPM < threshold in samples below min_samples are excluded. Leave empty to use default.

param.name: min_isoform_abundance
param.label: Minimum isoform abundance within gene
param.help: (Optional) Relative abundance threshold for isoforms within each gene (0-1 scale). Default: 0.05 (5%). Set to 0 to skip per-isoform filtering. For example, 0.05 means retain isoforms comprising >5% of the gene's total expression.

param.name: control_group
param.label: Control group for divergence
param.help: (Optional) Reference condition for divergence comparison. If empty, divergence computed between all condition pairs. Typically the untreated/normal condition.

param.name: nthreads
param.label: Number of threads
param.help: Number of CPU threads for parallel processing of entropy calculations and statistical tests (default: 1). Increase for faster computation on multi-core systems. Use 2-4 for typical laptops; 8+ for computing clusters. Each additional thread reduces runtime roughly proportionally.

param.name: output_prefix
param.label: Output file prefix
param.help: Prefix for output file names (default: tsenat)

param.name: save_intermediates
param.label: Save analysis object
param.help: Save the TSENATAnalysis object as RDS for downstream R analysis

param.name: enable_gene_subsetting
param.label: Enable gene subsampling
param.help: Enable selection of subset of genes based on variance, mean expression, or random selection. Useful for testing or reducing computational load on large datasets.

param.name: subset_n_genes
param.label: Number of genes to subsample
param.help: (Optional if gene subsampling enabled) Number of genes to retain from the filtered dataset. Genes selected according to subset_select_by criterion. Leave empty to use all genes.

param.name: subset_select_by
param.label: Gene selection method
param.help: Criterion for selecting genes when subset_n_genes is specified. Variance: highest within-condition variance (captures most dynamic genes). Mean: highest mean expression (captures most abundant genes). Random: random unbiased selection.

param.name: subset_seed
param.label: Random seed for subsampling
param.help: Seed for reproducible gene/sample selection (default: 42). Change to get different random subsets.

param.name: enable_m_estimator
param.label: Enable M-estimator advanced tuning
param.help: Enable advanced tuning parameters for M-estimator robust sample quality assessment.

param.name: m_estimator_loss_type
param.label: M-estimator loss function
param.help: Loss function type for robust M-estimation: Huber (default, balanced), Tukey (more robust), LSQ (least squares, less robust).

param.name: m_estimator_max_iter
param.label: M-estimator maximum iterations
param.help: Maximum iterations for M-estimation algorithm convergence (default: 50). Increase if convergence is slow on large datasets.

param.name: m_estimator_tol
param.label: M-estimator convergence tolerance
param.help: Convergence tolerance for M-estimation (default: 1e-6). Smaller values ensure higher precision but increase computational cost.

param.name: m_estimator_q_combine
param.label: Multi-q result combination
param.help: How to combine M-estimation results across multiple q-values: Mean (default, balanced) or Median (robust to outliers).

param.name: m_estimator_influence
param.label: Influence threshold
param.help: Threshold for classifying samples as high-influence (default: 0.75). Samples with influence score > threshold are flagged as potentially problematic.

param.name: m_estimator_scale_method
param.label: Scale estimation method
param.help: Method for estimating robust scale: MAD (default, median absolute deviation), Proposal 2, or S-estimator.

data.name: diversity_results
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Diversity q-spectrum
data.help: Tsallis entropy (diversity) values across q-spectrum for each gene and condition

data.name: sait_results
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: SAIT results
data.help: Statistical isoform switching significance (LM/GAM model p-values and estimates)

data.name: srh_results
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Scheirer-Ray-Hare test
data.help: Non-parametric rank-based test results for Q×Condition interaction

data.name: concordance_results
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Concordance (LM vs rank-based)
data.help: Agreement between parametric (SAIT/LM) and non-parametric (Scheirer-Ray-Hare) tests

data.name: effect_sizes
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Effect sizes
data.help: Rank-biserial and other effect size estimates

data.name: divergence_results
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Divergence results
data.help: KL/JS divergence between conditions across q-spectrum

data.name: jis_switching
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Jackknife isoform switching
data.help: Genes with detected coordinate isoform switching

data.name: qspectrum_plot
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Diversity q-spectrum plot
data.help: Diversity values across q-spectrum, colored by condition

data.name: influence_heatmap
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Isoform influence heatmap
data.help: Jackknife isoform relative influence on switching detection

data.name: divergence_distribution_plot
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Divergence distribution
data.help: Distribution of divergence values between conditions

data.name: divergence_spectrum_plot_top
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Top genes divergence spectrum
data.help: Divergence spectrum for top significant genes

data.name: top_transcripts_plot
data.label: TSENAT on ${on_string}: Top transcripts expression
data.help: Isoform-level expression patterns for top genes

tool.help:
**TSENAT: Tsallis Entropy Analysis Toolbox**
Quantify isoform-usage complexity in RNA-seq data using Tsallis entropy, detecting coordinate isoform switching and splicing-driven complexity changes that standard differential expression tools (DESeq2, edgeR) miss.
**Background** While traditional RNA-seq analysis measures transcript abundance changes, TSENAT measures the **diversity** of isoform distributions (entropy) at the gene level. This reveals: - Isoforms switching between conditions - Changes in usage distribution complexity - Genes with coordinated or antagonistic isoform patterns - Scale-dependent complexity via the parametric q parameter
**Key Concept: Tsallis Entropy**
Tsallis entropy uses a tuning parameter **q** (entropic index) that reveals scale-dependent complexity: - **q = 0.5**: Focuses on rare isoforms (sensitive to low-abundance variants) - **q = 1.0**: Shannon entropy (balanced view across all isoforms) - **q = 2.0**: Focuses on dominant isoforms (sensitive to major changes)
TSENAT computes a *spectrum* of entropy values (sweeping q from 0 to 2+) to characterize the full complexity landscape.
**Analysis Pipeline**
1. **Data Input & Filtering**: Accept transcript-level counts + metadata 2. **Diversity Calculation**: Compute Tsallis entropy across q-spectrum for each gene/condition 3. **Q-spectrum Visualization**: Plot diversity curves to visualize complexity patterns 4. **Statistical Testing**: Rank-based tests (Scheirer-Ray-Hare) to identify genes with significant Q×Condition interaction 5. **Effect Sizes**: Rank-biserial and other effect estimates 6. **Isoform Switching Detection**: Jackknife analysis to identify influencing isoforms 7. **Divergence Analysis**: Measure distributional differences between conditions 8. **Concordance Checking**: Compare parametric (LM/GAM) vs rank-based test agreement
**Inputs**
- **Transcript-level read counts**: Raw fragment counts (e.g., SALMON NumReads). Table with transcripts as rows, samples as columns. - **Sample metadata**: TSV with required columns: - `sample`: Sample identifiers (must match column names in counts table) - `condition`: Experimental condition/treatment group labels - `paired_samples` (optional): Subject/pairing ID for repeated-measures designs - **Transcript annotation**: GFF3 file with transcript→gene mapping (required for aggregation)
**Parameters**
- **Study Design**: Select "Paired" for repeated-measures designs (e.g., patient-matched samples) - **q-spectrum**: Define range and resolution for Tsallis entropy calculations. Default (0-2, step 0.05) covers most use cases. - Finer step size (0.01) = more resolution but slower - Coarser step size (0.1) = faster but less detail - **Filter Stringency**: Remove low-abundance transcripts that add noise - *Soft*: Keep ~90% of transcripts - *Medium* (default): Keep ~70-80% (recommended) - *Severe*: Keep only high-abundance transcripts (~50%)
**Outputs**
*Tables (TSV format)*: - **Diversity q-spectrum**: Entropy values by gene, condition, and q - **SAIT results**: Statistical isoform switching test (parametric LM/GAM model) - **Rank-based test**: Scheirer-Ray-Hare test for Q×Condition interaction - **Concordance**: Agreement between parametric and rank-based approaches - **Effect sizes**: Rank-biserial and other effect estimates - **Divergence results**: KL/JS divergence between conditions - **Isoform switching**: Genes with detected coordinate isoform changes (Jackknife-based)
*Plots (PDF format)*: - **Diversity q-spectrum curve**: Entropy trends across q, colored by condition - **SAIT heatmap**: -log10(p-value) matrix of switching isoforms - **Divergence visualizations**: Distribution plots and q-spectrum curves - **Influence heatmap**: Relative contribution of each isoform to switching detection - **Assumptions check**: Diagnostic plots for normality and rank-test assumptions
**Use Case Example**
When DESeq2 reports "no DE genes" but RT-qPCR suggests splicing changes: → Use TSENAT to detect isoform shifts instead of abundance shifts → Identify genes where isoform *distribution* changes significantly → Focus functional validation on coordinate switching patterns
**Citation**
If you use TSENAT in your research, please cite: [Publication details to be added]
**More Information**
- [TSENAT Bioconductor Package](https://bioconductor.org/packages/TSENAT) - [Galaxy Community](https://usegalaxy.org)
